title: A few questions
wikitext:
[]: A few questions             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Mon Feb 20, 2006 4:43 pm Instead of making a bunch of posts, I'll just put them all in one; 1) Why does wringing out a rag remind Shinji of a mother? 2) We all think that maybe Shinji and Rei could be a pair. But what I'm wondering is, instead of her affection for him being something like a crush, could it be more like motherly love? (Her being his mother's clone and all) 3) All the Eva units have souls in them. How did Nerv catch them? And I apologize if these are just some stupid questions with obvious answers.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Feb 20, 2006 5:03 pm 1) I think he's implying "good housewife and" along with "mother", Japanese society still being somewhat old-fashioned that way. 2) She looks like, and possibly even smells like (see also ep#14) his mother. But he also sees her as a girl his age, and is not averse to accidentally copping a feel (ep#5). Adding to the chaos, as Lilith, she is also a sort of adoptive grandmother. I think he's confused in his affections, like most of the other characters. But in the end, she, like Misato and Kaworu is another of the mothers whose apron strings he must in the end cut. 3) Lilith/ReiI was harvested from the debacle with Naoko. Yui went knowingly into contact with the unformatted EVA-01; and Kyoko knowingly with EVA-02 - and despite her precautions her maternal nature was snatched. NERV presumably selected the members of class 2-A amongst those whose mothers' souls they were able to harvest. And for the Eva Series, I suspect that SEELE would not be above another spot of human sacrifice (for the greater good, you understand).

[]: Re: A few questions             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 20, 2006 5:11 pm poetic_assassin wrote: 1) Why does wringing out a rag remind Shinji of a mother?

[]: Re: A few questions             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Feb 20, 2006 5:15 pm poetic_assassin wrote: Instead of making a bunch of posts, I'll just put them all in one; 1) Why does wringing out a rag remind Shinji of a mother?

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Mon Feb 20, 2006 7:32 pm Rei and Unit-00 are a special case, because Rei has no mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 20, 2006 8:08 pm I think the fact that Kaworu's special ability to sync with Evas cannot entirely be attributed to the fact that he is made of angelic material, which is similar to Rei. From ep24, Kaworu says: Eva is made of the same body as me. Because I am also the natus de Adamo. When the unit does not have a soul, I can unite. The soul of this unit is shutting itself up now.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Feb 21, 2006 3:15 am Eternal Yamcha wrote: You could also say that Rei I is pretty much the replacement of the mother's soul that "needs" to be present in an Evangelion. ... Rei isn't nearly as successful synching down with another Evangelion than Kaworu

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Feb 21, 2006 6:12 pm Unlike Rei, Kaworu has Adam's soul and is made from Adam, just like Evas were made from Adam. This can possibly explain why Rei doesn't exhibit the same ability to sync like Kaworu does, but is able to sync with Eva-00.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Feb 21, 2006 10:16 pm Eternal Yamcha wrote: Unlike Rei, Kaworu has Adam's soul and is made from Adam, just like Evas were made from Adam. This can possibly explain why Rei doesn't exhibit the same ability to sync like Kaworu does, but is able to sync with Eva-00.

[]: Re: A few questions             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 01, 2006 8:11 am poetic_assassin wrote: Instead of making a bunch of posts, I'll just put them all in one; 1) Why does wringing out a rag remind Shinji of a mother?

[]: Re: A few questions             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jul 01, 2006 8:32 am DatDude wrote: but heck lost of guys go looking for a girl just like mom if you know what im saying

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Jul 01, 2006 9:20 am Now you see why I am an Asuka Shinji shipper. Asuka is the only female Shinji shows any intrest in that isn't anykind of mother figure!

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sat Jul 01, 2006 8:32 pm You may have a point there.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 02, 2006 5:42 am Timesplitter 01 wrote: You may have a point there.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Jul 02, 2006 9:12 am Tines we are all little bastards at that age. Asuka's picking on Shinji could easly be seen as as crush. Shinji just seemed afraid of letting anyone in for the msot part

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Strangelove » Sun Jul 02, 2006 10:56 am Mr. Tines wrote: Timesplitter 01 wrote: You may have a point there.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 02, 2006 11:23 am Dr. Strangelove wrote: Adam is the *mother* of the Angels, and she was made from Adam. Physically, Karworu is male, but only physically...and even then, just barely.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Strangelove » Sun Jul 02, 2006 11:31 am Mr. Tines wrote: Dr. Strangelove wrote: Adam is the *mother* of the Angels, and she was made from Adam. Physically, Karworu is male, but only physically...and even then, just barely.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Jul 02, 2006 11:36 am It's very narrow-minded and arrogant to apply human gender to a god, the EVAs, Adam and Lilith, etc, are androgynous. The soul is genderless, as are you when your first created in the womb.

[]:             Quote Post by Six Winged Angel » Sun Jul 02, 2006 11:37 am yes, i prefer to say that they has no true gender anyway (well, except Rei)
summary: []: A few questions             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Mon Feb 20, 2006 4:43 pm Instead of making a bunch of posts, I'll just put them all in one; 1) Why does wringing out a rag remind Shinji of a mother? 2) We all think that maybe Shinji and Rei could be a pair. But what I'm wondering is, instead of her affection for him being something like a crush, could it be more like motherly love? (Her being his mother&#3
